summarization.a: The subfigure shows the hexagonal β-Si3N4 unit cell, illustrating the atomic arrangement of Si and N atoms along the crystallographic a and c directions.
summarization.b: The subfigure presents the bare β-Si3N4 (0001)-(1×1) surface, highlighting the surface atomic network and exposed nitrogen coordination.
summarization.c: The subfigure shows an H/NH2-terminated N-rich β-Si3N4 (0001) surface, identifying primary (N1°), secondary (N2°), and tertiary (N3°) amine groups bonded to surface nitrogen atoms.

a.question: How does the atomic arrangement shown in subfigure (a) reflect the crystalline structure of β-Si3N4?
a.answer: The unit cell shows a hexagonal crystal structure in which silicon and nitrogen atoms form a covalently bonded network. The ordered arrangement along the a and c lattice directions reflects the long-range periodicity that defines the bulk β-Si3N4 phase.

c.question: What types of amine groups are identified on the N-rich surface in subfigure (c)?
c.answer: Primary (N1°), secondary (N2°), and tertiary (N3°) amine groups.

c.question: Does subfigure (c) represent a chemically terminated surface compared to the bare surface in subfigure (b)?

b.question: Which crystallographic surface orientation is depicted in subfigure (b)?
